summary: 2026-06-05 08:48:10 PDT — The SSOT header change resolves the two prior findings: the branch no longer commits `Status: merged` before merge, and the remote PR merge no longer depends on an unpublished merged-status commit. AC1/AC4/AC9/AC10 are covered, but AC8 is still not met because the shipped tag is created and pushed after the merge as a separate operation.

findings.title: Shipped tag is not atomic with the merge
findings.claim: Step 5 says the annotated `shipped/<slug>` tag is created by the merge and that `no tag ⇒ not shipped`, but the remote path first runs `gh pr merge` at line 27 and only later creates and pushes the tag at lines 30-31; the local path likewise merges at line 36 and tags at line 37. If tag creation or push fails after a successful merge, the branch is shipped with no marker, so the AC9 derived check can report not shipped. This violates AC8's atomic shipped-marker requirement and breaks the SSOT guarantee for observed shipped state.
findings.suggestion: Either make the merge commit and `shipped/<slug>` ref update a single transactional operation, or change the spec/skill so it does not claim atomicity or `no tag ⇒ not shipped`; then add mandatory tag-push verification and an explicit repair path for a merged PR with a missing shipped tag.
